column1: # Tutorial Chapter 4: Learn (Record). The user designates a learn
# range with /Worksheet Learn Range, arms recording with Alt-F5, types
# real keystrokes (which also take effect in the worksheet), then
# disarms with Alt-F5 again. The recorded source materializes in the
# learn range as a label suitable for /Range Name binding to Alt-letter.
#   - /WLR opens a POINT prompt for the learn range (the recorder will
#     deposit captured source here when armed).
#   - Alt-F5 lights the LEARN status indicator while recording is on.
#   - Each pressed key is captured AND applied to the worksheet, so the
#     recording session is a real edit, not a dry run.
#   - Alt-F5 again disarms; LEARN clears; the captured source is
#     flushed into the learn range as a label, with `~` for ENTER and
#     `{KEY}` glyphs for arrow / function / control keys.
# --- Pick the learn range (B1..B3). POINT mode is entered after the
# typed cell is committed; `.` anchors and the second F5 GOTO extends.
ASSERT_PANEL_L3 Enter range to record into
# --- Reposition to A1 so the recorded keystrokes have a clean origin.
# --- Capture a tiny editing session: type a label, commit, move down.
# --- Disarm. The LEARN indicator clears and B1 receives the recorded
# source as a label. ENTER → `~`, arrow → `{DOWN}`.
# --- The recorded keystrokes also took effect live: A1 received "hi".
